title: Black and Red vertices of Tree

content.problemStatement:
You are given a tree with NNN vertices. The vertices are numbered from 111 to NNN. Some of the vertices are colored black and some are colored red. Also, some vertices can be uncolored. There is at least one black and at least one red vertex.

Compute the number of subsets of vertices WWW such that:


Each vertex in WWW is uncolored.
WWW is a connected subgraph of the tree.
If you remove all the vertices of set WWW, there will be at least one pair of vertices (u,v)(u, v)(u,v) such that uuu is black and vvv is red, and there is no path from uuu to vvv.
Output your answer modulo 109+710^{9} + 7109+7.


Input Format

The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN denoting the number of vertices.
Each of the next N−1N - 1N−1 lines contains two space-separated integers uuu and vvv denoting an edge between vertices uuu and vvv.
The next line contains NNN space-separated integers denoting the colors of the vertices. The ithi^{th}ith of these integers is 000 if vertex iii is uncolored, 111 if it's black, or 222 if it's red.


Output Format
For each test case, print a single line containing one integer denoting the number of ways to select a valid subset WWW, modulo 109+710^{9} + 7109+7.
constraints: Sum of N over all test cases doesn't exceed 10^6
content.inputFormat: The first line contains a single integer T denoting the number of test cases. Each test case starts with an integer N, the number of vertices in the tree. The next N-1 lines describe the edges of the tree, with each line containing two space-separated integers u and v denoting an edge between vertices u and v. The next line contains N space-separated integers denoting the colors of the vertices, where 0 represents an uncolored vertex, 1 represents a black vertex, and 2 represents a red vertex.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer denoting the number of ways to select a valid subset WWW, modulo 10^9 + 7.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Explanation: Test Case 1: The possible subsets WWW are {1}, {3}, {1,3}, {3,6}, and {1,3,6}. Test Case 2: The possible subsets WWW are {3} and {3,6}.

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we can use dynamic programming to calculate the number of ways to select a valid subset WWW for each subtree. We start by performing a depth-first search (DFS) on the tree to calculate the number of black and red vertices in each subtree. Then, for each vertex, we calculate the number of ways to select a valid subset WWW that includes the current vertex and the number of ways to select a valid subset WWW that does not include the current vertex. We use these values to calculate the total number of ways to select a valid subset WWW for the entire tree.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * N) - where T is the number of test cases and N is the number of vertices in the tree. The DFS traversal takes O(N) time, and we perform this traversal for each test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) - where N is the number of vertices in the tree. We need to store the colors of the vertices, the edges of the tree, and the dynamic programming values for each vertex.
